name: Pumpkin Spice Cake in Jars (Oven Method)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Freshly cooked and mashed pumpkin
recipeInstructions:
Do not use pumpkin pie filling.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease the inside of 8 - 1 pint wide-mouth Kerr or Ball canning jars or Ball Quality Crystal (12 oz.) with Crisco; set aside. The 1 pint jars are shorter but bigger around and hold more than the decorative 12 oz. jars, so use either one. They don't come with labels, so make your own.
Coarsely chop the raisins and walnuts; set aside.
Sift together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cloves, cinnamon, and ginger into a large bowl. Add raisins and walnuts; toss lightly to combine.
In another large bowl, beat eggs at high speed until thick and yellow (2-3 minutes). Gradually beat in sugar until thick and light. At low speed, beat in oil and pumpkin; blend well. Gradually stir in the flour mixture until well blended.
Divide mixture among 7 or 8 canning jars (should be slightly less than half full). Wipe the sides of the jar to prevent slops that may burn.
Place jars on a cookie sheet so they don't tip over.
Bake in a preheated 325°F oven for about 40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. When cakes are done, remove them one at a time and immediately place lids and rings on them.
Boil lids, rings, and jars for 10 minutes to sterilize before using. Keep lids in hot water until ready to use; then place them onto the jars.
If you prefer a regular cake:
Grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 50 minutes, or until done. If you like spicy cake, this will be one of your favorites.
